title: Torenia soriana
taxonomy: Torenia soriana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae, which is a family of small herbs and shrubs. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which includes many other flowering plants such as mint, honeysuckle, and foxglove. Torenia soriana is native to Asia and is also known as the "wishbone flower" due to its unique, heart-shaped blooms. The plant is often grown as an ornamental in gardens, valued for its delicate, blue-purple flowers and ability to thrive in a range of conditions. Torenia soriana is considered a perennial in its native habitat, but in warmer climates, it can also be grown as an annual.

intro: Torenia soriana, also known as Chinese bugloss or blue shellflower, is a type of flowering plant native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Torenia soriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Torenia soriana is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
